tasks.title: UI Structure Critique
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as routing changes

required_sections.name: Problem framing
required_sections.rationale: Sets the user-task and failure frame.

required_sections.name: Findings
required_sections.rationale: Surface real structural failures instead of taste comments.

required_sections.name: Recommendations
required_sections.rationale: Convert critique into concrete structural action.

required_sections.name: Tradeoffs
required_sections.rationale: Show what is preserved and what can flex first.

tasks.citation_schema: explicit threshold, measurable rule, or [File: Line] reference

service_level.accuracy: must identify the structural issue, not only aesthetic discomfort

required_decisions.description: Names the actual structural failure, not just taste discomfort.
Names what should win the first scan or primary action hierarchy.
Shows the order of interventions or ranked rebuild sequence.
Resolves clarity against another goal instead of keeping all goals equal.
Names what is directly observed vs inferred when visual input is part of the task.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses a hierarchy, flow, scan, or behavior rule.
Uses a real layout or density constraint.
Names a rejected alternative or competing pattern.
Uses a visual-structure rule when the critique is grounded in screenshots or visible layout evidence.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Cosmetic cleanup cannot stand in for structure.
Generic hierarchy language cannot pass without naming a winner.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not imply validation or proof without receipts.
Do not imply user evidence that is not actually present.

tasks.title: Component Specification
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as component state changes

required_sections.name: Purpose and scope
required_sections.rationale: Defines when the component exists and where it stops.

required_sections.name: Anatomy
required_sections.rationale: Names the governed parts.

required_sections.name: State matrix
required_sections.rationale: Shows interaction and edge states.

required_sections.name: Accessibility and implementation notes
required_sections.rationale: Prevents front-end drift.

tasks.citation_schema: token, prop, state, or [File: Line] reference

service_level.timeliness: spec should be implementable in one pass

required_decisions.description: Defines what the component owns and what remains outside it.
Makes state coverage explicit rather than implied.
Names keyboard, focus, aria, or announcement behavior.
Shows a token, prop, or implementation constraint.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses explicit state or behavior rules.
Uses component, token, or boundary constraints.
Uses aria, keyboard, or accessibility guidance when behavior depends on it.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: State logic cannot be deferred.
Accessibility cannot be postponed.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not call the component reusable without state-safe boundaries.

tasks.title: Dashboard Audit
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as KPI or data-source changes

required_sections.name: Dashboard role
required_sections.rationale: Classifies the view correctly.

required_sections.name: Key failures
required_sections.rationale: Names priority issues in hierarchy, density, and interpretation.

required_sections.name: Evidence and rationale
required_sections.rationale: Links recommendations to signal quality, KPI logic, or usage risk.

required_sections.name: Recommended rebuild path
required_sections.rationale: Shows the order of correction.

tasks.citation_schema: metric, threshold, benchmark score, or [File: Line] reference

service_level.accuracy: cannot misclassify operational vs executive dashboards

required_decisions.description: Classifies the dashboard role or user context.
Names the KPI or first-scan hierarchy winner.
Explains what should be compressed, grouped, or deferred.
Names filter, drill-down, or chart-choice logic.
Names what is directly observed vs inferred when visual input is part of the task.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses scan, interaction, or behavioral rules.
Uses KPI, threshold, or metric logic.
Compares chart or layout alternatives.
Uses a visual-structure rule when the critique is grounded in screenshots or visible layout evidence.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Adding more charts is not a hierarchy strategy.
Visual cleanup cannot replace KPI logic.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not claim efficiency gains without real metric logic.

tasks.title: Back-End Feasibility Review
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as auth or storage changes

required_sections.name: Requested capability
required_sections.rationale: States the visible ask and hidden system implications.

required_sections.name: Hidden system requirements
required_sections.rationale: Maps UI ask to auth, storage, exports, or APIs.

required_sections.name: Feasibility assessment
required_sections.rationale: Calls out blockers, assumptions, and sequencing.

required_sections.name: Safer implementation path
required_sections.rationale: Shows a realistic build order.

tasks.citation_schema: endpoint, schema field, permission rule, or [File: Line] reference

service_level.timeliness: must expose hidden architecture within one response
service_level.accuracy: must not hand-wave security or export implications

required_decisions.description: Names data model, schema, ownership, or retention implications.
Names auth, role, or access control implications.
Names storage, export, api, event, or realtime implications.
Names what blocks naive implementation or what must come first.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses a permission, auth, or ownership rule.
Uses schema, ownership, or data dependency logic.
Uses integration, sequencing, or system constraints.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: The task cannot be reframed as visual-only.
Feasibility cannot be waved through.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not imply safety or scalability without actual constraints.

tasks.title: PDF Remediation Plan
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as remediation tooling or standards changes

required_sections.name: Current failure state
required_sections.rationale: Names the real accessibility/document integrity problem.

required_sections.name: Remediation sequence
required_sections.rationale: Orders the steps so semantics are preserved.

required_sections.name: Verification checks
required_sections.rationale: Shows how success is confirmed.

required_sections.name: Risk controls
required_sections.rationale: Prevents destructive edits and copy-paste damage.

tasks.citation_schema: standard, extraction check, or [File: Line] reference

service_level.accuracy: must separate cosmetic from semantic fixes

required_decisions.description: Names structure, tagging, or semantic failure state.
Names reading-order or extraction fidelity concerns.
Defines how remediation success is checked.
Rejects flattening, rasterizing, or OCR-first shortcuts.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses tagging, accessibility, or document standards references.
Uses explicit verification checks.
Uses preservation and sequencing constraints.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Destructive repair cannot be the main fix.
The plan cannot blur semantics into visual cleanup.
patterns: looks correct visually so it is fine

overclaim_rules.description: Do not call the file compliant or accessible without verification.

tasks.title: Brand Positioning Pass
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as audience or market evidence changes

required_sections.name: Audience and problem
required_sections.rationale: Anchors brand to a real segment and pain.

required_sections.name: Positioning frame
required_sections.rationale: Shows category, alternative, and wedge.

required_sections.name: Trust and proof burden
required_sections.rationale: Prevents adjective-only branding.

required_sections.name: Messaging consequences
required_sections.rationale: Translates strategy into language behavior.

tasks.citation_schema: segment fact, proof artifact, or [File: Line] reference

service_level.timeliness: should resolve who it is for and why now

required_decisions.description: Names the segment and the problem or perception gap.
Names what alternative the brand is being positioned against.
Names the trust signal or proof burden.
Translates the strategy into message or tone behavior.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses alternative, category, or competitor comparison logic.
Uses a proof artifact, signal, or file-backed receipt when available.
Uses a real constraint such as trust, usability, or category convention.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Adjective stacks cannot stand in for positioning.
Tone advice cannot float without audience logic.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not imply superiority without real proof.

tasks.title: Case Study Rewrite
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as new proof artifacts

required_sections.name: Problem
required_sections.rationale: Names the original problem and stakes.

required_sections.name: Process
required_sections.rationale: Shows what was actually done, not generic steps.

required_sections.name: Solution
required_sections.rationale: Explains the rebuilt logic or artifact.

required_sections.name: Outcome and proof
required_sections.rationale: Links claims to evidence and open gaps.

tasks.citation_schema: metric, quote, benchmark score, or [File: Line] reference
hard_fail_patterns: this case study shows my passion

service_level.timeliness: must surface the rebuilt structure first
service_level.accuracy: cannot imply validation beyond the evidence

required_decisions.description: Separates the project claim from the available proof.
Distinguishes proxy or internal proof from measured or external proof.
Explains the structural order or why it changed.
Resolves narrative smoothness against evidentiary honesty.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses benchmark, before/after, or alternative comparison logic.
Uses explicit claim-to-proof boundary language.
Uses benchmark files, maps, or proof artifacts when available.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Storytelling cannot replace proof calibration.
A findings-only rewrite does not satisfy the task.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not imply external validation without external artifacts.

tasks.title: Accessibility Feedback Audit
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as interaction pattern changes

required_sections.name: Access failure framing
required_sections.rationale: Name the blocked user action or assistive-tech failure.

required_sections.name: Barrier inventory
required_sections.rationale: List concrete barriers, not generic usability discomfort.

required_sections.name: Repair priorities
required_sections.rationale: Turn findings into an ordered fix plan.

required_sections.name: Verification method
required_sections.rationale: Show how the fix will be verified.

tasks.citation_schema: WCAG/APCA rule, keyboard/focus behavior, or [File: Line] receipt

service_level.accuracy: must name the blocked behavior, not only visual discomfort
service_level.freshness: must reflect current interaction pattern rules

required_decisions.description: Names the blocked or degraded user action.
Shows what should be fixed first.
Names the behavior rule, not only the visual preference.
Explains how to verify the repair.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses WCAG, APCA, ARIA, or accessibility semantics.
Uses focus, keyboard, or state behavior rules.
Names how the repair will be tested.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Accessibility cannot be postponed until later.
Color-only fixes cannot stand in for semantic repair.
patterns: change the color and it is fixed

overclaim_rules.description: Do not claim compliance without a standard or test receipt.

tasks.title: Color System Specification
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as theme or semantic-role changes

required_sections.name: Role model
required_sections.rationale: Defines semantic color roles instead of a loose palette.

required_sections.name: Token map
required_sections.rationale: Maps roles to tokens, aliases, or state usage.

required_sections.name: Contrast and state rules
required_sections.rationale: Prevents color-only semantics and contrast drift.

required_sections.name: Migration notes
required_sections.rationale: Shows how to move from the current palette safely.

tasks.citation_schema: token role, contrast threshold, state rule, or [File: Line] receipt

service_level.accuracy: must separate palette taste from role behavior
service_level.freshness: must reflect current theme and status usage

required_decisions.description: Defines role ownership such as surface, text, status, action, or chart roles.
Maps roles to states or interaction use.
Names contrast or non-color fallback requirements.
Shows how existing colors migrate into the new role model.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses token, alias, or theme-boundary constraints.
Uses contrast thresholds or measurable limits.
Names a rejected palette-only alternative or previous state.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: A color list without semantic roles cannot pass.
Status meaning cannot rely on color alone.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not call the color system accessible without thresholds or fallback rules.

tasks.title: Graphic Critique
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as campaign or editorial artifact changes

required_sections.name: Communication goal
required_sections.rationale: Defines what the artifact needs to communicate first.

required_sections.name: Composition failures
required_sections.rationale: Names focal, hierarchy, or distance-legibility failures.

required_sections.name: Rebuild moves
required_sections.rationale: Turns critique into compositional action.

required_sections.name: Distance and emphasis tradeoff
required_sections.rationale: Shows what should dominate and what can recede.

tasks.citation_schema: visual rule, compositional threshold, or [File: Line] receipt

service_level.accuracy: must diagnose composition, not only style preference
service_level.freshness: must reflect current campaign or artifact constraints

required_decisions.description: Names what should dominate the first read.
Names distance or scan-legibility implications.
Uses alignment, grouping, rhythm, or proportion language.
Resolves what can recede so the focal message survives.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses composition, focal, rhythm, or legibility rules.
Names the rejected alternative or competing focal path.
Uses size, crop, or production-boundary constraints.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Taste commentary cannot replace communication diagnosis.
Style comments without a communication goal cannot pass.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not claim readability gains without a legibility rule or testable reason.

tasks.title: Layout Reconstruction Plan
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as source-artifact updates

required_sections.name: Source constraints
required_sections.rationale: Defines what must be preserved from the source artifact.

required_sections.name: Reconstruction assumptions
required_sections.rationale: Makes inferred structure explicit instead of pretending certainty.

required_sections.name: Rebuild sequence
required_sections.rationale: Shows the ordered reconstruction plan.

required_sections.name: Verification checkpoints
required_sections.rationale: Protects against drift and silent geometry errors.

tasks.citation_schema: geometry rule, inferred assumption, or [File: Line] receipt

service_level.accuracy: must separate source truth from inferred structure
service_level.freshness: must reflect the latest source artifact

required_decisions.description: Names what geometry or content must remain stable.
Labels inferred structure as assumption or proxy.
Shows the ordered reconstruction sequence.
Names how the reconstruction will be checked.
Names what is directly observed vs inferred when visual input is part of the task.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses geometry, grid, or preservation constraints.
Compares the rebuilt artifact back to the source.
Uses overlays, measurements, or extraction checks.
Uses a visual-structure rule when the critique is grounded in screenshots or visible layout evidence.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Redrawing from scratch ignores the reconstruction brief.
Eyeballed spacing cannot stand in for structure.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not claim exact preservation without measured comparison or explicit inference labeling.

tasks.title: Type System Recommendation
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as type scale or readability rules

required_sections.name: Reading context
required_sections.rationale: Defines where the type system will be read and at what density.

required_sections.name: Scale and role map
required_sections.rationale: Maps text roles to scale, weight, and hierarchy rules.

required_sections.name: Readability rules
required_sections.rationale: Names line length, contrast, spacing, or emphasis boundaries.

required_sections.name: Adoption guidance
required_sections.rationale: Shows how to phase the new type system into the artifact.

tasks.citation_schema: scale rule, readability threshold, or [File: Line] receipt

service_level.accuracy: must separate reading context from taste
service_level.freshness: must reflect current density and accessibility expectations

required_decisions.description: Defines the reading context and density constraint.
Maps roles such as display, heading, body, label, or meta text.
Uses a readability rule or threshold.
Shows what changes first and what stays stable.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses legibility, hierarchy, or reading-flow rules.
Uses measurable type or readability thresholds.
Uses token, density, or platform-boundary constraints.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: A font swap is not a type system.
Taste language cannot replace readability rules.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not claim readability is solved without measurable rules or context.

tasks.title: UX Research Gap Map
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as the product scope or audience assumptions

required_sections.name: Known evidence
required_sections.rationale: Separates what is already known from assumption.

required_sections.name: Critical gaps
required_sections.rationale: Names the highest-risk unknowns that block good decisions.

required_sections.name: Research plan
required_sections.rationale: Maps each gap to a method, sample, and output.

required_sections.name: Decision impact
required_sections.rationale: Shows what product or narrative decision each study unlocks.

tasks.citation_schema: research artifact, sample rule, or [File: Line] receipt

service_level.accuracy: must distinguish current evidence from assumption
service_level.freshness: must reflect the latest product scope and audience frame

required_decisions.description: Separates known evidence from assumption.
Ranks which research gaps matter first.
Maps gaps to concrete research methods.
Connects the study back to a product or communication decision.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses research methods, participants, or artifacts.
Compares current evidence to missing evidence or alternative methods.
Distinguishes assumption, proxy evidence, and measured proof.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Generic 'do interviews' advice cannot pass.
Unranked wish lists are not research plans.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not imply user evidence that has not been collected.

tasks.title: Front-End Implementation Review
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as rendering, state, or interaction architecture changes

required_sections.name: Architectural framing
required_sections.rationale: Name the real front-end architectural problem and its user-facing consequence.

required_sections.name: Boundary and state model
required_sections.rationale: Make server/client and state ownership explicit.

required_sections.name: Rendering and mutation strategy
required_sections.rationale: Choose rendering, fetching, and mutation handling instead of generic implementation advice.

required_sections.name: Risks and safer path
required_sections.rationale: Expose the cost surface and safer implementation order.

tasks.citation_schema: rendering rule, state model, accessibility contract, or [File: Line] receipt

service_level.accuracy: must make rendering, boundary, and state decisions explicit
service_level.freshness: must reflect current server/client and interaction architecture expectations

required_decisions.description: Chooses the rendering strategy instead of implying it.
Names where state lives and how transitions are handled.
Makes the server/client boundary or mutation boundary explicit.
Names the semantic or native-element contract.
Names the cost surface or degraded path.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses rendering or boundary-specific evidence.
Uses explicit state or interaction behavior rules.
Uses real implementation constraints rather than generic framework advice.
Uses semantic or accessibility standards when behavior depends on them.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: A framework swap cannot stand in for an architectural decision.
More hooks is not an architectural answer.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not call the implementation production-ready without boundary, state, and cost logic.

tasks.title: Back-End Architecture Spec
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as authority, consistency, or delivery-model changes

required_sections.name: System framing
required_sections.rationale: Names the system, actors, resources, and why architecture is needed.

required_sections.name: Core model and authority boundaries
required_sections.rationale: Defines ownership, authorization, and source-of-truth.

required_sections.name: Data, consistency, and delivery design
required_sections.rationale: Chooses consistency, pagination, events, async, or webhook patterns.

required_sections.name: Observability and failure posture
required_sections.rationale: Shows how the system will be monitored and where it can safely degrade.

tasks.citation_schema: authority rule, consistency model, delivery pattern, or [File: Line] receipt
hard_fail_patterns: use uuid and it is secure

service_level.accuracy: must resolve authority and delivery choices rather than naming technologies only
service_level.freshness: must reflect current consistency and authorization expectations

required_decisions.description: Names actors, resources, and actions.
Names the source-of-truth or ownership model.
Names the authorization posture.
Names the freshness or consistency model.
Names pagination, async, event, or webhook design.
Names metrics, tracing, or operational tax.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses data-model or ownership evidence.
Uses authorization or policy evidence.
Uses explicit consistency or delivery-model evidence.
Names operational or scalability constraints.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Endpoint count is not architecture.
Identifiers do not replace authorization.
patterns: use uuid and it is secure

overclaim_rules.description: Do not call the system scalable or enterprise-ready without naming the architecture tax and authority model.

tasks.title: API Reliability and Security Review
tasks.freshness_expectation: same release cycle as API contract, auth, or background-job changes

required_sections.name: Failure semantics
required_sections.rationale: Defines the error envelope and what the client can reason about.

required_sections.name: Authorization and resource protection
required_sections.rationale: Names BOLA/BFLA style boundaries and resource controls.

required_sections.name: Idempotency and async lifecycle
required_sections.rationale: Makes retry safety and long-running job behavior explicit.

required_sections.name: Resilience and observability
required_sections.rationale: Names resilience, quotas, degradation, and telemetry posture.

tasks.citation_schema: RFC rule, auth policy, lifecycle contract, or [File: Line] receipt

service_level.timeliness: single-pass API reliability and security review
service_level.accuracy: must define retry, auth, and failure semantics instead of generic security advice
service_level.freshness: must reflect current API and agentic workload risks

required_decisions.description: Names RFC 9457 style failure semantics.
Names the authorization boundary and relevant API risk.
Names idempotency-key behavior.
Names the async job lifecycle when work is long-running.
Names resilience or protection rules.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses structured failure-semantic evidence.
Uses authorization or access-control evidence.
Uses idempotency or async lifecycle evidence.
Uses resilience, quota, or observability evidence.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: Retries without idempotency are not safe.
Failure cannot hide inside a success envelope.

overclaim_rules.description: Do not call the API secure or reliable without explicit contracts.

tasks.title: Text Humanization Revision
tasks.freshness_expectation: same session as source revision requests

required_sections.name: Job of the piece
required_sections.rationale: Identifies what the text must still do after revision.

required_sections.name: Pattern scan
required_sections.rationale: Shows what felt machine-shaped or repetitive.

required_sections.name: Meaning-preservation guard
required_sections.rationale: Protects against semantic drift or tone replacement.

required_sections.name: Revised passage
required_sections.rationale: Shows the rewritten prose.

required_sections.name: Why this reads more human
required_sections.rationale: Explains the revision logic instead of asserting it.

tasks.citation_schema: pattern note, meaning guard, or [File: Line] receipt

service_level.timeliness: single-pass prose revision with meaning guard
service_level.accuracy: must preserve argument and claims unless the user requests a structural rewrite
service_level.freshness: should reflect the current source text and user voice

required_decisions.description: Names what the text is trying to do.
Names repeated or machine-shaped patterns.
Names what must remain stable.
Makes revision logic visible.
Protects the writer's tone from generic polish.

required_evidence_classes.description: Uses explicit meaning-preservation language.
Uses concrete prose-quality evidence like repetition, rhythm, or verb logic.
Uses before/after or explicit revision comparison logic.

forbidden_shortcuts.description: The rewrite cannot exist only to look different.
Casualization cannot stand in for humanization.
patterns: make it more casual by default

overclaim_rules.description: Do not claim the revision is more human without naming meaning guard and revision logic.
